blocks.text: Draft me a confidentiality clause for a Series B term sheet between a UK manufacturing company and a US lead investor. Standard market terms; mutual; 3-year survival.

blocks.thinking:
The lawyer wants a confidentiality clause for a Series B term sheet. This is a drafting request - they're asking me to produce text, not review or redline an existing document.

Let me check the Turn 1 discovery protocol:
- Noun: confidentiality clause (or term sheet)
- On-demand playbooks: Neither RFQ nor NDA specifically matches "confidentiality clause"
- Skills: There's no skill that specifically names "confidentiality clause" or "term sheet" - closest would be NDA review but that's for reviewing, not drafting
- Batch: Single item, no delegation needed

This is a drafting task. I should draft the clause in plain text since there's no document to edit. I'll also update the todo.

The specs:
- UK manufacturing company
- US lead investor
- Series B term sheet
- Mutual confidentiality (both sides)
- 3-year survival on termination/expiry

Standard market terms for a Series B term sheet confidentiality clause:
- Mutual obligation
- Definition of confidential information
- Exclusions (publicly available, independently developed, lawfully received, prior knowledge, required by law)
- Permitted disclosure to advisors bound by confidentiality
- Standard term (indefinite or specific) with survival
- Return/destruction of confidential information
- No license or rights granted
- Injunctive relief (equitable remedy) - common in VC term sheets

Let me draft this concisely.

arguments.content:
- [ ] Draft mutual confidentiality clause for Series B term sheet
- [ ] UK manufacturing company (disclosing party) / US lead investor
- [ ] 3-year survival on termination/expiry
- [ ] Confirm draft with lawyer
